title: In Group Discussions: - Ask Questions:
subtitle: Challenge the Consensus
description:
In group discussions:
- Ask questions: "Have we considered other perspectives?"
- Encourage dissent: Create a safe space for alternative viewpoints.
- Pause before deciding: Suggest taking time to explore overlooked options.

Example: Before agreeing with a team’s rushed decision, propose reviewing potential risks and alternatives.

faq.question: How do I track my progress with this habit?
faq.answer: Use the check-in method described above and track it in the Life OS app for consistent progress monitoring.

faq.question: What if I miss a day or forget to do this habit?
faq.answer: Don't worry! Life OS habits are designed to be flexible. Just get back on track the next day without judgment.

faq.answer: Research shows habits can take 21-66 days to form. Focus on consistency rather than perfection, and use the app to build momentum.

howTo.title: Define the metric baseline
howTo.summary: Identify the current number of alternative perspectives raised in decisions and the average time spent in structured pauses. This will serve as your baseline for measuring improvement.

howTo.title: Add a pause to your next meeting
howTo.summary: In your next meeting invite, include a line item for a 3-5 minute pause to consider alternative perspectives. This sets the expectation for structured dissent.

howTo.title: Practice the dissent script
howTo.summary: Before the meeting, rehearse a concise script to ask about alternative perspectives. Use a neutral tone to invite discussion without confrontation.

howTo.title: Implement the silent write
howTo.summary: During the pause, have participants write down their concerns or alternatives silently for 3 minutes. Collect and discuss these ideas to enhance decision quality.

howTo.title: Check in with LifeOS Brali app
howTo.summary: Log your results in the Brali LifeOS app. Record the number of alternatives raised and the time spent in structured pauses at https://metalhatscats.com/life-os/challenge-groupthink-in-meetings/.
